hypothesis: If an EfficientNet-B0 CNN is first pretrained on a large additive-free NCM SEM dataset and then fine-tuned on the small, class-imbalanced additive-induced SEM dataset with oversampling or weighted loss, then it will achieve higher class-wise accuracy and F1 in classifying NCM composition and aging state than models trained only on the target data or only on the source data, because transferable microstructural features learned from the source domain generalize to the additive-induced domain.
